channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Tehran Is Defiant After Trump Threatens Power Plants
item.description: Iran dismissed President Trump’s ultimatum to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz, saying it would consider electric plants and water facilities “legitimate targets” if its electrical grid were struck.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: French Far Right Falls Short of Statement Win in Yardstick Local Races
item.description: France’s far right hoped for major gains in Sunday’s municipal elections, a key bellwether moment before a presidential election next year. Its results were mixed.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler and Catherine Porter
item.media:description: Casting a ballot on Sunday in Lyon, southeastern France.

item.title: Israel’s Missile Defense Under Scrutiny After Iranian Attack
item.description: Two missiles landed hours apart, wreaking havoc in two towns near a heavily guarded nuclear site in the Negev Desert.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: Peering Sunday at damage where a missile struck the night before in Arad, Israel.

item.title: On Beirut’s Waterfront, Loss Meets Life, and Luxury, Amid War
item.description: Thousands displaced by Israeli strikes on Lebanon’s capital now shelter along the promenade hugging the Mediterranean Sea. They share it with joggers, cyclists and dog walkers, alongside dizzying displays of wealth.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Diego Ibarra Sanchez
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A displaced man in Beirut, Lebanon, sitting near the sea. More than a million people have been displaced in the country since the latest conflict between Israel and Hezbollah began.

item.title: The Billionaire Funding France’s Far Right
item.description: Pierre-Édouard Stérin is financing projects to make France less Muslim, more Catholic and more capitalist. He says his program has trained thousands running for municipal office on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter
item.media:credit: James Hill for The New York Times
item.media:description: Pierre-Édouard Stérin at his chalet in the French Alps on Wednesday. He moved his family to Belgium in 2012.

item.title: Attack on a Sudan Hospital Kills Dozens, Head of W.H.O. Says
item.description: The group’s director general said 13 children were among those killed in the latest violence in the Darfur region, and he decried the targeting of health care facilities in the civil war.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.media:description: Refugees waiting to be registered at the Oure Cassoni camp in Chad after fleeing the civil war in Sudan, in February. The war has created a devastating humanitarian crisis.

item.title: Italy’s Leader Wants to Change the Constitution. Italians Don’t Get It.
item.description: Voting starts Sunday on a referendum to overhaul Italy’s judiciary. Many people don’t understand the complex proposal — and it could hurt Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich and Josephine de La Bruyère
item.media:description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy at a campaign event this month.

item.title: Terror Attacks Haunted This Brussels Community. Now It’s Trying to Move On.
item.description: Molenbeek was branded a hotbed of radicalization after terrorists hit Brussels and Paris a decade ago, but new challenges show that reinvention doesn’t come easy.
item.dc:creator: Koba Ryckewaert and Jeanna Smialek
item.media:credit: Jim Huylebroek for The New York Times
item.media:description: Graffiti on one of the bridges over the canal dividing Molenbeek and the city of Brussels this month.

item.title: Election in Slovenia Is a Near Tie
item.description: The center-left and the right-wing populists got about equal support. The next government will be led by the one who can build a coalition with smaller parties.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins
item.media:description: Voters casting their ballots in Radomlje, Slovenia, on Sunday.

item.title: With Fire and Song, Kurds in Turkey Hail the Start of Spring
item.description: The Newroz holiday comes at a critical time, as Kurds in Turkey push for peace while their brethren in Syria and Iran fear further conflict.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard, Safak Timur and Rena Effendi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Kurds celebrating Newroz, an ancient holiday that marks the start of spring in the northern hemisphere, in Diyarbakir, Turkey.

item.title: Patriarch Filaret Dies at 97, Orthodox Church of Ukraine Says
item.description: Filaret led a movement in Ukraine that created a schism among Orthodox believers and, ultimately, a church independent of Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd
item.media:credit: Nicole Tung for The New York Times
item.media:description: Patriarch Filaret, center, at St. Volodymyr’s Cathedral in Kyiv, Ukraine, in June 2022. President Volodymyr Zelensky called him “one of the most steadfast defenders” of Ukrainian statehood.

item.title: No Pills or Needles, Just Paper: How Deadly Drugs Are Changing
item.description: Lab-made drugs soaked into the pages of letters, books and even legal documents are being smuggled behind bars, killing inmates and frustrating investigators.
item.dc:creator: Azam Ahmed, Matt Richtel and Meridith Kohut
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Division 9 Maximum Security Dormitory of the Cook County Department of Corrections — where inmates have been found using deadly illicit drugs — saturated onto paper — in the pages of books, letters from family and legal documents.

item.title: Zelensky Sends Negotiators to the U.S., Hoping to Revive Peace Talks
item.description: The Ukrainian president has said he has a “very bad feeling” about the effects of the Iran conflict on Ukraine’s own war.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine on Wednesday in Madrid. He has called for the resumption of peace talks, which have lost momentum in recent weeks.

item.title: The Road to the Battle of Hastings in 1066? Maybe Not a Road After All.
item.description: New research suggests that a legendary 200-mile march by Harold Godwinson, the last Anglo-Saxon king of England, may have actually involved some ships.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.category: Harold II, King of England (Harold Godwinson)
item.media:description: Re-enactors setting out from Clifford’s Tower in York, England, to follow the supposed journey of King Harold to the site of the Battle of Hastings, for the 950th anniversary of the battle in 2016.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Julian Barnes, Isabel Kershner and Yan Zhuang

item.title: Caught Between Two Conflicts, Afghans Flee Iran
item.description: The war in Iran is choking off Afghanistan’s main economic lifeline and has forced at least 70,000 Afghan workers and students there back home to a nation embroiled in another conflict.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Kiana Hayeri
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: People crossing the border from Iran to Afghanistan last week at Islam Qala.

item.title: In Denmark’s Election, How Will the Woman Who Took On Trump Fare?
item.description: Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen is the biggest force this country has seen in decades. The crisis in Greenland has energized her, but are voters itching for change?
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman and Maya Tekeli
item.media:credit: Hilary Swift for The New York Times
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen greeting constituents during a campaign event on Tuesday in her hometown Aalborg, Denmark.

item.title: Iranians Fear Trump’s Threat to Strike Power Plants
item.description: With President Trump threatening to strike Iranian power plants, some war-weary civilians are panicking over a possible new debacle.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran residents on Sunday, when tensions were rising over whether power would be cut to the capital and home to 10 million people.

item.title: Trump’s Ultimatum to Iran
item.description: The president’s messages on the war have shifted, but the latest threat might be a sign that he’s moving to a harder stance.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Sunday
item.description: The Israeli military said its campaign against Hezbollah in Lebanon would intensify, while Iran threatened to attack civilian infrastructure if President Trump followed through with an ultimatum.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: Iran Responds to Trump’s Energy Threat With Defiance and Warnings of Its Own
item.description: Tehran “will not hesitate in defending its people and its land,” a senior official said, after President Trump threatened to destroy Iranian power plants.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iran’s first vice president, Mohammad Reza Aref, center, in February.

item.title: Israel Orders Military to Intensify Demolitions in Southern Lebanon
item.description: Israel Katz, the defense minister, said he ordered troops to destroy more bridges and buildings in southern Lebanon, stoking worries that Israel was widening a military-controlled buffer zone there.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cars turn around on a bridge in southern Lebanon after an Israeli strike destroyed it on Sunday.

item.title: Residents in Southern Israel Are Shaken After Direct Strikes by Iranian Missiles
item.description: Attacks on Saturday injured dozens in Arad and Dimona, two cities closest to Israel’s main nuclear research facility.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: Emergency workers at the site of a missile strike in Dimona, Israel, on Sunday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Julian Barnes and Isabel Kershner

item.title: France Chose Thousands of New Mayors on Sunday. Here’s Why It Matters.
item.description: The far-right party had first-round successes in several cities, but so did the far left. Sunday’s second round may clarify who has real momentum.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.media:credit: James Hill for The New York Times
item.media:description: Campaign posters outside a school in Marseille, France.

item.title: Who Is Antigone? The 2500-Year-Old Rebel With a Cause.
item.description: “Antigone” gave us the original “bad girl,” but its themes go beyond that. How do adaptations keep making Sophocles’ ideas about democracy and theater new?
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Saturday
item.description: An Iranian missile wounded dozens in Dimona, a city believed to house Israel’s nuclear weapons program, as Iran showed no signs of backing down.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An Israeli soldier uses a flashlight while walking in the ruins of a damaged building in the southern Israeli city of Dimona.

item.title: Iranian missile strikes Israeli city near main nuclear research facility.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:description: Emergency workers approach a site where Iranian missiles struck Dimona, Israel on Saturday.

item.title: Internet Blackout Keeps Iranians From Reaching Loved Ones During Nowruz Holiday
item.description: The current restrictions on the internet in Iran are coinciding with Nowruz, the observance of the Persian New Year.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Shoppers in Tehran prepare for Nowruz, the Persian New Year, even as travel difficulties and an internet blackout keep families apart.

item.title: How the Litani River Shapes the Israel-Hezbollah War
item.description: The 90-mile waterway, back in focus amid Israel’s renewed offensive in Lebanon, could define how far the fighting spreads.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Damage to a bridge over the Litani river in southern Lebanon after it was struck on Wednesday.

item.title: Umberto Bossi, Fiery Founder of Italian Separatist Party, Dies at 84
item.description: A charismatic populist, he channeled the anger of a disaffected North, arguing — in inflammatory oratory — for secession and railing against a tide of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo
item.media:description: Umberto Bossi in 2008. He appealed to disaffected northerners who saw Italy’s Rome-centered, “colonialist” national government as having robbed them of their hard-earned money and their cultural identity.

item.title: K-Pop’s BTS Returns to the Stage
item.description: The group’s first concert in nearly four years — held in the historic center of Seoul — was heavy on songs from its new album. They also played a few old hits.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Jun Michael Park for The New York Times

item.title: Israel Strikes Across Lebanon Amid Fierce Ground Fighting in the South
item.description: The Israeli military said it was stepping up attacks aimed at infrastructure in Beirut belonging to Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Damaged buildings in the southern neighborhoods of Beirut, the capital of Lebanon, on Saturday, after an Israeli airstrike.

item.title: Middle East War Leaves Hamas Balancing Allies on Opposite Sides
item.description: The Palestinian militant group, which has ties to both Iran and Qatar, is “walking on a knife’s edge,” an analyst said.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon and Abu Bakr Bashir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Armed members of Hamas in Gaza City in November.

item.title: Iranian Man Arrested After Trying to Enter U.K. Naval Base, Police Say
item.description: The man and a Romanian woman walked up to the gate of the base in Scotland, where nuclear-armed submarines are docked, and asked to be let in, an official said.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: One of Britain’s nuclear-powered submarines undergoing maintenance at H.M. Naval Base Clyde in Scotland in 2023.

item.title: The BTS Comeback Concert is Taking Place in the Heart of Seoul. But Not Everyone is Happy.
item.description: As South Korea rallies around the much-hyped comeback concert for the global superstars, some are questioning whether too many public resources are being used for a corporate production.
item.dc:creator: Max Kim
item.media:credit: Jun Michael Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gwanghwamun Square in Seoul on Saturday.

item.title: Spring Awakening
item.description: Astronomical spring began yesterday, but the actual change of season is more gradual, a halting and nonlinear progression.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: Canada Shifts From Peacekeeping to Building a Strong Military
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s latest global tour emphasized military cooperation rather than developing trade ties.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A Norwegian tank at a NATO winter training exercise in Badufoss, Norway, this month.

item.title: These BTS Fans Waited 15 Hours to See Their Idols
item.description: Those without tickets arrived at the venue before the sun rose on Saturday to stake out areas that offered unobstructed views of the stage.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.media:description: International BTS fans waiting for the concert on Saturday, from left: Thycianne Santos, Corrine van der Moolen, Leimomi Kamiya, Betty Pietraszora, Kayla Sanchez, Niki Anchala and Emi Noguchi.

item.title: Iranian Sailors, Including Survivors of Torpedoed Ship, Remain in Limbo
item.description: Sri Lanka has kept more than 250 sailors in protective custody since the early days of the war. Iran wants them back, but Sri Lanka is unsure what to do with them.
item.dc:creator: Anupreeta Das and Pamodi Waravita
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Iranian Navy supply ship Bushehr getting assistance off the coast of Colombo on March 5, a day after another one, the Dena, was torpedoed by a U.S. submarine, in a photo provided by the Sri Lankan Navy.

item.title: Fidel Castro’s Grandson Flaunts Beer, Nikes and Trump Jokes on Instagram
item.description: Sandro Castro has drawn scrutiny for his Instagram posts in which he flaunts a life of luxury while using satire to point out the very deterioration his family’s leadership helped create.
item.dc:creator: Maria Abi-Habib and Emiliano Rodríguez Mega

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar, Aaron Boxerman, Julian E. Barnes, Eric Schmitt and David E. Sanger

item.title: Chelsea Chief Under Abramovich Could Face Scrutiny Over Soccer Cheating
item.description: A former key aide to the Russian oligarch would face an investigation into her time overseeing the Premier League club should she return to soccer.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.media:description: Marina Granovskaia before a match at Wembley Stadium in London in 2022, when she was a Chelsea board member.

item.title: A Public Holiday Every Week? Sri Lankans Bemoan the Enforced Day Off.
item.description: The island’s government, rationing fuel amid shortages from the Iran war, surprised residents by imposing an unusual four-day week.
item.dc:creator: Pamodi Waravita and Anupreeta Das
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A vendor waiting for customers at a railway station in Colombo, Sri Lanka, on Wednesday, after the government announced a weekly holiday on Wednesdays to save fuel.

item.title: ​At Least 14 Killed in South Korean Factory Fire
item.description: Survivors jumped from windows to escape the blaze, the deadliest at a factory in South Korea in two years. Four people are still missing.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun
item.media:description: Firefighters spraying water to extinguish a fire at a factory in Daejeon, South Korea, on Friday.

item.title: Trump Signals Retreat From Goal of Regime Change in Iran
item.description: In the first days of the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran, Mr. Trump pressed for a popular uprising against Tehran’s government.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People at an apartment building that was hit by U.S.-Israeli airstrikes in Tehran last week.

item.title: U.S. Pauses Sanctions on Some Iranian Oil to Get More to Market
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent estimated that the move would add about 140 million barrels of crude to the oil market.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said in a post on X that Iran would see little economic benefit from the temporary removal of the sanctions.

item.title: A Look at the Escalating Battle for the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: There are several ways the United States could attempt to reopen the waterway, all of which carry substantial risks.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The village of Kumzar, along the Strait of Hormuz in Oman.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in Iran in Its Third Week
item.description: The Persian new year and the end of the holy month of Ramadan were marked by continuing strikes in the region as the war reached the end of its third week.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Muslims celebrate Eid al-Fitr at the Mohammad Al-Amin Mosque in Beirut, Lebanon, on Friday.

item.title: U.S. Attacks Iranian Targets to Try to Open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Israeli military said it had killed the spokesman for Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, and Iran warned that it could target American and Israeli military personnel.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Eric Schmitt, Francesca Regalado and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iran has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz to most Western ships, helping to drive up oil prices and rocking the global economy.

item.title: Switzerland Blocks Arms Sales to U.S. Over Iran War
item.description: Swiss law prohibits selling war materiel to countries involved in armed conflict, a policy the country has already cited to block sales to Israel and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Nick Cumming-Bruce
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The United States is one of the biggest markets for Swiss weapons exports, including small arms.

item.title: U.K. Allows U.S. to Use Bases to Hit Iranian Forces Menacing Strait Traffic
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has vowed not to be dragged into the war with Iran, but his government described its new position as essentially defensive.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A B-1 bomber at a Royal Air Force base in Fairford, England, on Sunday.

item.title: Energy facilities have been a potent target in the war.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Elliott, Blacki Migliozzi, Lazaro Gamio, Christiaan Triebert and Emmett Lindner

item.title: Stone Fragment Is Found in Wreck of Ship That Carried Parthenon Marbles
item.description: Experts said the fragment, recovered by divers from the Greek culture ministry, matched the style and dimensions of the Parthenon, but that it was too soon to be certain of its provenance.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai
item.media:description: Divers have found a marble fragment from the wreck of the Mentor, a ship that sank in 1802 while carrying Greek antiquities.

item.title: Aid Ship Departs for Cuba as Island Grapples With a Fuel Blockade
item.description: The “Nuestra América” humanitarian convoy plans to deliver more than 20 tons of critical supplies to Cuba. Some Cuban exiles view it with suspicion.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Paul Antoine Matos
item.media:description: People watching the sunset in Havana on Monday during a blackout.

item.title: A Few Ships Are Trickling Through the Strait of Hormuz With Iran’s Approval
item.description: Countries and companies have been negotiating with the Iranian authorities to secure passage. Some patterns are emerging.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo ships sailing toward the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of the United Arab Emirates on Thursday.

item.title: Denmark Was Ready to Blow Up Airfields to Stop a U.S. Invasion of Greenland
item.description: The Danes brought blood supplies, explosives and live ammunition to Greenland as part of contingency plans in case President Trump acted on his threats to seize the island.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman, Christopher F. Schuetze and Maya Tekeli
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: According to a report in Danish public media, Nuuk Airport in Greenland was one of two airports that the Danish military was planning to disable in the event of a U.S. invasion.

item.title: Shigeaki Mori, Hiroshima Survivor Photographed With Obama, Dies at 88
item.description: He lived through the first atomic bombing in Japan and then spent decades researching the identities of 12 American P.O.W.s killed in the attack.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Shigeaki Mori with President Barack Obama in 2016. The first American president to visit Hiroshima, Mr. Obama paid tribute to Mr. Mori, “a man who sought out families of Americans killed here, because he believed their loss was equal to his own.”

item.title: Trump Officials Bypass Congress to Sell Weapons to U.A.E., Kuwait and Jordan
item.description: Some of the proposed sales, valued at more than $23 billion, were under review, while others were never sent to Congress. The administration is pushing them through without congressional approval.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio determined that “an emergency exists that requires the immediate sale” of weapons to the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait and Jordan, according to his department.

item.title: Iran’s New Supreme Leader Issues a Statement but Remains Unseen
item.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei has remained out of the public’s sight since assuming the post, amid reports that he was badly wounded.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei succeeded his father as Iran’s supreme leader earlier this month but has not appeared in public or on video since then.
